figures.caption: Figure 3: Distribution of patch complexity metrics (files modified, hunks, changed lines) across five benchmarks (SWE-PB: SWE-PolyBench; SWE-MM: SWE-bench Multimodal; Multi-SWE: Multi-SWE-bench; Rust-SWE: Rust-SWE-bench; Ours: MobileDev-Bench). Each data point is the per-repository average. Y-axes are capped at the 90th percentile for visual clarity.

Figure 7: Percentage of instances per framework whose fix patches involve each technical knowledge domain, inferred from import statements, annotations, and identifiers in the added lines of fix patches. An instance may span multiple domains. React Native percentages are indicative (n=14).

Table 1: Comparison of issue-resolution benchmarks. Unlike prior work that primarily evaluates localized sourcecode fixes in library-style repositories, MobileDev-Bench targets mobile apps, incorporates human verification and difficulty stratification, evaluates multi-artifact fixes (e.g., source and resources artifacts such as manifest files), and restores pre-fix buildability to enable execution-based evaluation.
Table 11: Distribution of artifact type combinations in fix patches. 35.7% of instances require coordinated changes across two or more artifact types.
Table 10: Framework-wise instance distribution and code change characteristics in MobileDev-Bench
Figure 1: Overview of MobileDev-Bench construction pipeline.
Table 9: Benchmark repository instance conversion pipeline showing progression from collected pull requests through attribute-based filtering, execution-based validation, and manual verification.
Figure 4: Recall decreases sharply as the number of modified files increases.
Table 13: Resolution rate breakdown by task characteristics. Shows distribution of resolved instances (39 total unique across all models) compared to overall benchmark distribution (384 tasks).
Table 12: Resolution rates by ground truth file count. Shows number of resolved instances and resolution rate percentages for each model, grouped by file count bins.
Table 8: Summary and licenses for all GitHub repositories represented in MobileDev-Bench.

Table 14: Retrieval metrics by ground truth file count. All metrics are percentages, averaged across tasks in each file count bin. Column abbreviations: C=Claude Sonnet 4.5, Q=Qwen3 Coder, G=GPT 5.2, Ge=Gemini 2.5 Flash.

Figure 11: Retrieval metrics (recall, precision, F1) by ground-truth file count across all four models. Recall collapses from 34–53% on single-file tasks to 2–3% on 11+ file tasks, while precision remains comparatively stable (43–68%), widening the precision-recall gap with complexity. F1 degrades sharply, driven entirely by recall failure.

Table 3: Per-repository statistics for MobileDev-Bench. #Files and #LoC report codebase size at the base commit. # is the instance count. #Tok is the average issue description length in tokens. Fix patch columns (#L: changed lines, #H: hunks, #F: files) and test transition columns (F2P: Fail-to-Pass, N2P: None-to-Pass, P2P: Pass-to-Pass) report per-instance averages.
